user_story: As Chip, I can invoke chip-supergoal with /chip-supergoal, SG, plan-and-ship, standing continuation, or risky refactor wording.
expected_behaviour: The skill is selected for non-trivial planning and standing SuperGoal continuation/repair; tiny direct tasks are refused as too small for SG.

user_story: As Chip, I can ask for a SuperGoal package without the planner executing implementation phases.
expected_behaviour: Planner may inspect/research/write artifacts, but must not perform numbered implementation phase execution.

user_story: As Chip, I get exactly one human-facing launch body.
expected_behaviour: Only LAUNCH_GOAL.md contains an actual line starting SUPERGOAL_GOAL_BODY:; ROADMAP/THINKING do not hide alternate launch bodies.

user_story: As Chip, I can launch one standard /goal that reads STATE.md and continues to audit.
expected_behaviour: Launch card uses upstream-compatible /goal contract; no chained /goal bodies.

user_story: As Chip, I get a complete disk-backed .supergoal package.
expected_behaviour: Package includes THINKING, optional RESEARCH, LOOP_DESIGN, ROADMAP, STATE, PROTOCOL, LAUNCH_GOAL, phase specs, scripts/repo-state.sh, and delivery scripts when requested.

user_story: As an executor, I can validate every phase before running it.
expected_behaviour: Every phase has SUPERGOAL_PHASE_START metadata, Work, Acceptance criteria, Mandatory commands, Evidence required headings; validate-phase.sh fails malformed specs.

user_story: As Chip, every plan gets a senior review before handoff.
expected_behaviour: RPD_PLAN_REVIEW runs in Stage 6 and findings mutate artifacts or are marked checked-holds with evidence tier.

user_story: As Chip, risky work gets explicit senior gate coverage.
expected_behaviour: Auth/payments/secrets/prod/migrations/gateway/cron/model/public/destructive phases set RPD required yes with focus and evidence.

user_story: As Chip, SG cannot claim done before audit.
expected_behaviour: Final completion re-reads ROADMAP, verifies deliverables, runs aggregate checks, emits RPD_FINAL_REVIEW, AUDIT_COMPLETE, then SUPERGOAL_RUN_COMPLETE.

feature: Telegram delivery as blocking done criterion
user_story: As Chip, requested Telegram files are real attachments with receipts.
expected_behaviour: Review/final file delivery creates receipts and verifies ok=true/sent=true before claiming gate complete.

user_story: As Chip, SG asks only where it matters.
expected_behaviour: Only Stage 1 material clarifying questions and Stage 6 go/no-go are human gates by default.

user_story: As Chip, planner uses the live loaded skill, not stale paths.
expected_behaviour: Stage 0 resolves live skill_dir and records skill path/context notes before trusting old locations.

user_story: As Chip, intake is concise but sufficient.
expected_behaviour: Brownfield asks 0–2 questions; greenfield batches up to 4 until material gaps close; retrievable context is gathered with tools.

user_story: As Chip, roadmap starts from actual repo state.
expected_behaviour: Stage 2 runs stack/env/repo scripts and records stack, commands, and risks.

user_story: As Chip, current facts and existing solutions are checked before design.
expected_behaviour: Research runs when current facts matter and writes RESEARCH.md with sources, candidates, verdict, implications, and unverified claims.

user_story: As Chip, design choices are explicit before phases.
expected_behaviour: Architecture tradeoffs/decision phases are documented for architecture/evaluation SGs; heavyweight candidates get eligibility gates.

user_story: As Chip, work is sliced by verifiable units, not arbitrary phase count.
expected_behaviour: Planner creates as many phases as required with dependencies, acceptance criteria, commands, evidence, and no fixed cap.

user_story: As an executor, I can resume from STATE.md.
expected_behaviour: STATE.md records current phase, baseline ref, engineering check status, live status snapshot, events, delivery state.

user_story: As an evaluator, I can parse SG lifecycle markers.
expected_behaviour: PROTOCOL preserves SUPERGOAL_PHASE_START/STATUS/VERIFY/DONE/TURN_YIELD, PREFLIGHT, RPD, FAILURE, AUDIT, delivery, and completion markers.

user_story: As Chip, /goal keeps going through all safe phases until final audit or a real blocker.
expected_behaviour: GoalManager must not stop at numbered phase boundaries; SUPERGOAL_TURN_YIELD is only a forced-yield/blocker marker

user_story: As Chip, continuation prompts do work instead of just reporting status.
expected_behaviour: If STATE.md is not DONE/BLOCKED, executor continues current phase/audit from disk and does not answer only with status.

user_story: As Chip, stale post-complete wrappers do not spam the chat.
expected_behaviour: If STATE/current phase is complete, loop stops; repeated identical post-complete continuations get at most minimal status/silence.

user_story: As Chip, real human/provider approval blockers pause cleanly.
expected_behaviour: BLOCKED_BY_APPROVAL/READY_FOR_DELETE_APPROVAL causes a stop without re-running checks or restating cards every continuation.

user_story: As Chip, failures are probed and handed off explicitly.
expected_behaviour: Failures emit FAILURE_PROBE/FAILURE_ESCALATE/FAILURE_HANDOFF and do not get converted into false completion.

user_story: As Chip, packages are checked before dispatch.
expected_behaviour: Stage 6.5 verifies baseline commands, repo state, required files, blockers and emits PREFLIGHT_GREEN or PREFLIGHT_RED.

user_story: As Chip, executor can show changed deliverables against baseline.
expected_behaviour: repo-state.sh reports name-status and dirty state for deliverable paths; ignored .supergoal is handled separately.

user_story: As Chip, git-clean does not hide stale/ignored package drift.
expected_behaviour: Planner/executor reports ignored .supergoal artifacts separately and directly verifies package files before claiming clean.

user_story: As Chip, skill edits are validated before being reported.
expected_behaviour: Changed skills parse frontmatter, keep root concise, load linked files, run tests/guards, scan for secrets, and skill_view loads.

user_story: As Chip, private GoalManager patches survive updates or are reduced safely.
expected_behaviour: Reconciliation records HEAD/branch/status, compares upstream/private split, runs focused matrix, and reports remote/local boundary.

user_story: As Chip, GoalManager/gateway regressions get the correct review checklist.
expected_behaviour: When reviewing/fixing Hermes GoalManager, gateway launch, Telegram clarify button, or structured completion, reference dispatch loads code-review hardening.

user_story: As Chip, an RPD/xhigh verdict can become a clean SG package.
expected_behaviour: Quoted RPD verdict is treated as source; stale .supergoal residue is not mixed into the new package.

user_story: As Chip, launch UX is short, file-first, and verifiable.
expected_behaviour: Planner sends/records allowed review files, presents one launch card, and live verification checks visible GoalManager state when starting.

user_story: As Chip, rich Telegram formatting does not break /goal launch.
expected_behaviour: Markdown launch pipeline preserves required /goal text, avoids malformed entities, and verifies active state rather than send logs.

user_story: As Chip, button-started goals attach to the visible topic/session.
expected_behaviour: Callback/session handling verifies group/topic GoalManager state active; wrong sibling/forum sessions are migrated/paused.

user_story: As Chip, review files are delivered only to allowed surfaces.
expected_behaviour: Review pack is exactly four md files by default — THINKING

user_story: As Chip, final artifact sends are deduped and auditable.
expected_behaviour: Final files are packaged/sent once with receipt; retries do not create duplicate/conflicting delivery claims.

user_story: As Chip, prod-affecting SGs require explicit safety rails.
expected_behaviour: Rollback, approval manifests, service status, logs, process integrity, and deploy smoke are required for production-adjacent work.

user_story: As Chip, gateway restart fixes are proven live without creating bogus goals.
expected_behaviour: Restart proof includes service status, logs, visible GoalManager state, and guards against accidental goal text from assistant reports.

user_story: As Chip, reports do not merge sibling SG states.
expected_behaviour: Status/audit lookup resolves the exact goal/package and does not combine results from similar sibling goals.

user_story: As Chip, progress reports are readable but not false proof.
expected_behaviour: SUPERGOAL_STATUS includes phase, percent, action, check summary, evidence, next step; completion still requires audit markers.

user_story: As Chip, repeated incidents become gates/probes rather than prose bloat.
expected_behaviour: Dev incident classes map to invariant/check/regression; probe script validates key contract references.

user_story: As Chip, SG remains compatible with upstream /goal semantics.
expected_behaviour: Probe validates safe /goal compatibility markers and launch template assumptions.

user_story: As Chip, planner records available tools/auth without leaking secrets.
expected_behaviour: detect-env reports relevant command/auth availability while avoiding credential dumps.

user_story: As Chip, planner summarizes repo stack and dirty/remote status.
expected_behaviour: detect-stack identifies languages/package managers/git branch/remote/dirty state.

user_story: As Chip, planner gets a compact repo file/language overview.
expected_behaviour: summarize-repo outputs bounded repository summary suitable for THINKING/ROADMAP evidence.

user_story: As maintainer, I can reference old behavior without reintroducing root bloat.
expected_behaviour: Legacy monolith is kept for historical archaeology only, not used as primary live instructions.

user_story: As Chip, SGs involving auth UX require safe readable errors.
expected_behaviour: Auth UX phases map server errors to safe copy, accessible status surfaces, and dark-theme legibility checks.

user_story: As Chip, OAuth/provider SGs verify exact provider config.
expected_behaviour: OAuth audits verify redirect URIs, provider status, credentials boundaries, and no invented routes.

user_story: As Chip, manually-created provider resources are adopted instead of duplicated.
expected_behaviour: Planner/executor discovers existing resources, records identifiers, and avoids unsafe recreate/delete without approval.

user_story: As Chip, financial/live activation goals separate safe checks from live rails.
expected_behaviour: Financial/live rail tasks require explicit approvals, read-only verification first, and precise status language.

user_story: As Chip, approvals are external/material, not agent-internal stalling.
expected_behaviour: SG does not invent internal approval blockers; asks once only for bounded external manifest when needed.

user_story: As Chip, compressed GoalManager state remains resume-safe.
expected_behaviour: Migration verifies compression tip, load_goal status, and active/paused/blocked semantics after change.

user_story: As Chip, continuation recovers from wrong/stale package roots.
expected_behaviour: Executor resolves current package root, anchors context, and quarantines/avoids wrong-goal residue.

user_story: As Chip, final audit does not loop forever or claim early.
expected_behaviour: Completion incidents are guarded by explicit audit evidence, terminal state, and final marker order.

user_story: As a maintainer, I can understand the skill from repo README.
expected_behaviour: README explains purpose, package artifacts, validation, and usage without private runtime residue.
